purpose: Enable manual installed-extension QA before any HTML, manifest, or JS milestone.

steps.title: Open Chrome extension management
steps.instruction: Open Chrome and navigate to chrome://extensions.

steps.title: Enable Developer mode
steps.instruction: Turn on Developer mode in the top-right of the extensions page.

steps.title: Open the sidepanel
steps.instruction: Pin or open the extension action, then open the Chrome side panel for the NODAL OS lab extension.

steps.title: Open DevTools for the sidepanel
steps.instruction: Inspect the sidepanel page if Chrome exposes the extension view, then check the console for critical errors.

steps.title: Validate tabs
steps.instruction: Open Operar, Aprender, Recetas, and Runtime. Capture one screenshot per tab.

steps.title: Validate narrow width
steps.instruction: Resize the sidepanel or browser window to approximately 420px wide and confirm STOP remains visible.

steps.title: Validate keyboard focus
steps.instruction: Use Tab and Shift+Tab to move through controls. Confirm the focus ring is visible and not hidden.

steps.title: Validate governance surfaces
steps.instruction: Confirm consent, handoff, human banner, runtime, model, and blocked-state surfaces read as governed and non-active.

steps.title: Validate logs
steps.instruction: Confirm log pane and preformatted blocks read as auxiliary diagnostics, not product evidence or source of truth.

doNotDo: Do not edit extension files during QA.
Do not connect model or cloud services.
